Content inside is left-aligned and may be structured into two vertical sub-columns: the left 60–65% for office name, address, and general description, and the right 35–40% for prominent phone and email details, mirroring the logo/contact split in the site header. The top of the panel aligns visually with the page title (if present) and the content runs horizontally in a way that naturally leads the eye from important contact details on the left to reinforced details on the right. Below the panel, a 24–32px vertical gap clearly separates it from the inquiry-type tab selector.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard block-level element in normal document flow, scrolling with the page and defining the top edge of the main content column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The inquiry-type-selector sits directly beneath the main-office contact panel with roughly 24px of vertical spacing. It spans the same centered content column width as the panel above, forming a shallow horizontal band of tabs. The selector is a flat bar about 48–56px tall, with each tab occupying equal horizontal space so that the row fills roughly 90–100% of the content column width. Tabs are rectangular, with minimal rounding or none at all, consistent with the sharp, classic styling of the mockup. The active tab is highlighted with a solid red background and white text, possibly with a subtle black bottom border or slight elevation to emphasize its selection. Inactive tabs use a light grey background with black or dark grey text. There is a thin dividing line beneath the tabs (dark grey on white) that visually anchors this component and delineates it from the form content below. A small 16–24px vertical gap separates the bottom of the tabs from the feedback panels or, when those are hidden, directly from the form header/first fields.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard block-level component in normal flow; tabs behave as horizontal navigation within the form area but do not stick on scroll.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The design-build-project-section lives inside the main inquiry form and appears as a distinct block when the selected inquiry type requires project details (for example, when a 'Design-Build Project' tab is active). Within the form, it sits below the contact-details-section and above the message-section, separated from adjacent sections by approximately 24–32px of vertical space. The section spans the full width of the centered content column (same as the form), with a clear, left-aligned section heading in bold black text and a thin grey underline or generous top margin to distinguish it. Inside, fields are arranged in a two-column grid on desktop: for example, project type and project location might sit side by side in the top row, with project budget and desired start date below, each column occupying about 50% of the available width with 24px horizontal gutter. Larger controls like a project description textarea or file upload row span the full width of the section and sit beneath the two-column rows. The section uses 16–24px vertical spacing between its internal rows, keeping the feel compact but not cramped. When this section is hidden (for non-project inquiry types), its space collapses completely, allowing the message-section to move up directly beneath the contact-details-section.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Contained within the inquiry-form as part of the normal vertical flow; its visibility is toggled based on the selected tab, with the layout collapsing or expanding without leaving empty space.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Within the inquiry form, the contact-preferences-section is placed near the bottom, typically after the message-section and just above the final submit controls. It spans the full width of the form’s content column and is separated from the preceding section by about 24px of vertical space. A left-aligned section label in bold black text introduces this block, possibly with a subtle grey subtitle explaining preferences. Inside, content is arranged in one or two horizontal rows of options, such as preferred contact method and best time to reach the visitor. On desktop, radio or checkbox groups may be arranged in two columns, each column occupying around 50% of the width, with 24px of horizontal spacing between them, echoing the 12-column grid structure. Each preference option line is compact, with 8–12px padding around labels and about 16px vertical spacing between rows, reflecting the moderately dense yet readable style. The whole section is visually lighter than the project or contact details sections, acting as a final fine-tuning block before submission. A small 16–24px vertical gap separates the bottom of this section from the submit button row.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard section within the inquiry-form, scrolling with the form content and maintaining its relative position just above the form’s action buttons.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The contact-details-section is the first major block inside the inquiry-form and appears directly beneath any feedback panel (or directly under the tab selector if no feedback is visible). It is separated from the component above by about 16–24px, and from the next section below by 24–32px. The section spans the full width of the centered content column, with a left-aligned heading like 'Your Contact Details' in bold black text. Fields inside follow a structured, grid-like pattern reminiscent of a corporate form: on desktop, the top row typically places 'First Name' and 'Last Name' side by side, each at 50% width with approximately 24px of horizontal gutter, aligning to a 6/6 split in a 12-column grid. The next row might place 'Email' and 'Phone' in the same two-column configuration. Additional fields (company name, role, etc.) can either share row space or span full width depending on importance. Vertical spacing between individual rows is around 16–24px, with labels kept tight to their respective inputs. The layout is dense but not cluttered, using flat white backgrounds, thin grey input borders, and sparse red indicators only for required fields or errors.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Anchored near the top of the inquiry-form’s normal flow, this section scrolls with the form and serves as the first, most immediate interaction area within the form.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The message-section sits within the inquiry-form beneath the design-build-project-section (when present) or directly beneath the contact-details-section (when the project section is hidden), separated from the section above by about 24–32px. It spans the full width of the form’s content column and is oriented vertically. A bold, left-aligned heading such as 'Message' or 'Project Details' appears at the top of the section, sometimes accompanied by a short grey helper line explaining what to include. Below this heading, a large textarea occupies almost the full width of the column with a flat white background and a thin grey border; its height is visually substantial, equivalent to 3–4 lines of standard input fields, so that the section itself consumes a noticeable chunk of vertical space. Supporting controls like an optional subject line or attachment link may appear in a single full-width row above the textarea. Internal vertical spacing is 16–24px between the label, helper text, and the textarea, ensuring readability while keeping the composition compact. A gap of roughly 24px separates the bottom of this section from the contact-preferences-section.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Part of the sequential vertical flow within the inquiry-form, expanding naturally based on the height of the textarea and scrolling with the rest of the form content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The inquiry-form is the main structural container for all input-related components and occupies the central portion of the white content area. It begins just below the feedback region that follows the inquiry-type tabs, with about 16–24px of vertical spacing. The form’s outer width matches the centered content column—about 70–75% of the viewport width, maxing out at around 1100–1200px—and it is horizontally centered with generous white space to the left and right. The form content is stacked vertically in this order: the contact-details-section at the top, followed by the design-build-project-section (when applicable to the selected tab), then the message-section, then the contact-preferences-section, and finally the submit controls row at the bottom. Each section is separated by 24–32px of vertical spacing, maintaining clear groupings. Inside each section, a consistent two-column grid is used for most fields, with each column taking roughly 50% of the width and 24px horizontal gutter, collapsing to a single column on narrow viewports to maintain readability. The bottom of the form ends with a horizontal row containing the primary submit button aligned to the right edge of the form column, possibly accompanied by a secondary action (such as reset) aligned to the left or immediately left of the primary. The submit button is a strong flat rectangle, typically red with white text, sized at about 44–48px in height and padded 16–24px horizontally, reinforcing its importance. Overall, the form is dense but disciplined, echoing the corporate, grid-based aesthetic of the mockup.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard block-level container in the main content flow, scrolling with the page; internally, it governs the stacking order and responsive behavior of all form sections and feedback panels.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The form-validation-messages panel occupies a reserved slot between the inquiry-type-selector and the top of the inquiry-form. When there are validation errors or warnings, it appears as a full-width rectangular alert aligned with the centered content column, about 16–24px beneath the tabs and 16px above the first form section. The panel spans the same width as the form and uses a flat white or very light red-tinted background with a solid red left border or top border approximately 3–4px thick to create a strong but controlled emphasis. Inside, content is left-aligned and may be structured as a short summary line in bold black or dark grey text, followed by a bulleted list of specific errors in a smaller font. Vertical padding of around 16–20px ensures the text is readable, while the panel’s overall height remains relatively compact (about the same as one to two rows of form fields). When no validation messages are present, this panel collapses completely, and the inquiry-form moves up to sit directly beneath the tab selector or any other visible feedback panel.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Appears and disappears within the normal document flow, pushing the inquiry-form down when visible; it does not overlay other content and scrolls with the page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The submission-result-panel shares the same horizontal slot as the form-validation-messages, positioned directly beneath the inquiry-type-selector and above the inquiry-form. After a successful submission (or a critical failure), it appears as a full-width feedback card aligned with the centered form column, sitting approximately 16–24px below the tabs. The panel is slightly more prominent than the validation messages: for success, it may use a white background with a strong black border and a red accent line or icon; for failure, it may mirror the error styling with a red-tinted background and bold black text. The panel’s width matches the form column, and its internal content is left-aligned with 16–24px padding. A clear heading (e.g., 'Thank you for your inquiry') in bold black text sits at the top, possibly followed by smaller explanatory text on next steps. The panel’s height is moderate—roughly 60–120px depending on message length—giving it enough presence to visually replace the form as the primary focus without feeling like a separate page. The form below may remain visible but visually secondary, or in some implementations may be collapsed or dimmed; in either case, the result panel occupies the top feedback position and pushes any remaining form content downward.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Appears in the normal vertical flow directly under the tabs, pushing the form content downward. It scrolls with the page and may temporarily replace the validation-messages panel in this slot to avoid stacking multiple feedback blocks.

name: About Us
title: About Us
description: Page that retrieves and presents company background information (overview, history, mission, values, statistics, certifications, and leadership profiles) using the getAboutPageContent interface, with simple in-page navigation and a path to contact the company.

core_features: Load company background data (overview, history, mission, values, stats, certifications, leadership) by calling getAboutPageContent when the page initializes or when the user refreshes the data.
Display company overview, history, and mission as separate readable sections, each directly bound to the corresponding fields from getAboutPageContent.
Display company values as a list where each value item (title and description) is rendered from the values array returned by getAboutPageContent.
Display company statistics as a structured summary bound to the stats array returned by getAboutPageContent, allowing users to quickly scan key metrics.
Display certifications as a list or grouped section, bound to the certifications array from getAboutPageContent, including name, issuing body, and description.
Display the leadership team as an interactive directory bound to the leadership_team array from getAboutPageContent, allowing users to select a person to view more detailed information.

Provide in-page navigation that lets users jump to specific sections (overview, history, mission, values, stats, certifications, leadership) and reflects the selected section in the URL via a section query parameter.
Provide a leadership profile focus state (e.g., expanded panel or detail view) that can be controlled via URL parameters (e.g., leaderId) so users can link directly to a specific leadership profile.
Expose a 'Contact us' call-to-action that navigates users to contact.html with the URL parameter inquiry_type=general_inquiry when clicked.
user_workflows: Viewing the company background: 1) User navigates to about.html (no specific query parameters). 2) The page-level data loader component calls getAboutPageContent. 3) Once data is returned, the overview, history, mission, values, stats, certifications, and leadership sections render using the response fields. 4) The in-page navigation component reads any existing section query parameter (if present); if none, it sets a default section as active (e.g., overview) without altering the content. 5) The user scrolls the page or uses in-page navigation to jump between sections.

Jumping directly to a specific section via URL: 1) User lands on a URL such as about.html?section=leadership (section parameter value is not hardcoded in logic, it is interpreted generically). 2) The page-level data loader calls getAboutPageContent. 3) After the data is loaded, the in-page navigation component reads the section query parameter and sets the corresponding section as active/highlighted. 4) The page scrolls or focuses to the matching section. 5) If the user clicks another section link in the in-page navigation, the section query parameter in the URL is updated accordingly and focus/scroll updates.

Deep-linking to a specific leadership profile: 1) User opens a link to about.html?section=leadership&leaderId={some-leadership-identifier-derived-from-data}. 2) The data loader calls getAboutPageContent and receives the leadership_team array. 3) The leadership directory component reads leaderId from the URL and searches the leadership_team array to find the matching entry (based on a derived ID, index, slug, or other identifier computed consistently on both link creation and parsing). 4) If a match is found, that leader is set as the currently selected profile; the detail panel component displays that leader’s full bio using the bound data. 5) If the user selects a different leader by clicking another leadership item, the URL leaderId parameter is updated and the focused profile changes.

Using in-page navigation to explore sections: 1) User is on about.html with content loaded from getAboutPageContent. 2) In the in-page navigation component, the user clicks a navigation item (e.g., mission, values, or stats). 3) The click handler updates the section query parameter in the URL using the History API (or a full reload), and optionally scrolls into view the target section. 4) If a full reload occurs, getAboutPageContent is called again and the in-page navigation reads the updated section parameter to re-focus correctly. 5) The user can continue clicking different sections and the URL remains in sync with the currently active section.

Viewing a specific leader’s details from the list: 1) User scrolls to or navigates to the leadership section. 2) The leadership directory component lists all leadership_team items from getAboutPageContent. 3) The user clicks one leadership item. 4) The click handler sets that person as selected in component state and updates the leaderId query parameter (and section=leadership if not already set). 5) The leadership detail component renders full information for the selected leader using the selected leadership_team entry’s fields (name, title, bio, photo_url). 6) If the user reloads the page, the leadership detail component again reads leaderId from the URL and restores the same selection.

Navigating to the contact page from About: 1) From any section within about.html, the user sees a 'Contact us' call-to-action component. 2) The user clicks this CTA. 3) The click handler performs a navigation to contact.html with the query parameter inquiry_type set to general_inquiry (contact.html?inquiry_type=general_inquiry). 4) The contact page reads this parameter to pre-select or configure the general inquiry mode (handled on the contact page, not within the About page).
interactions: Page load: onInit event triggers the getAboutPageContent interface call. While waiting, components may show loading states (functional only, not visual design). Once data is received, all bound sections render updated content.

URL parameter parsing: onInit and onURLChange events read query parameters such as section and leaderId. These values determine which section is active and whether a leadership profile is selected when the page first renders or when the URL changes.
In-page navigation click: onClickSectionLink updates the section query parameter in the URL and scrolls or focuses the relevant section, without changing any underlying data from getAboutPageContent.

Leadership item click: onSelectLeader maps the clicked item to its corresponding entry in leadership_team, sets it as the current selection in local component state, and synchronizes the URL by setting or updating the leaderId query parameter (and ensuring section is set to the leadership section).
Contact CTA click: onClickContactCTA triggers navigation to contact.html with inquiry_type=general_inquiry as a query parameter, without modifying any data returned from getAboutPageContent.

Manual URL editing or link-based navigation: When users navigate to about.html with different section or leaderId query parameters (e.g., via bookmarks or shared links), the onURLChange logic re-applies active section and leader selection based on those parameters after getAboutPageContent data has loaded.
page_functionality.state_logic: The About page uses URL query parameters to control view state instead of local storage. The primary parameters are: (1) section: indicates which section should be considered active or scrolled into view (e.g., overview, history, mission, values, stats, certifications, leadership). The in-page navigation component and the main layout both read this parameter on page load and URL changes; when a user clicks a navigation item, the component updates the section parameter to the corresponding section key. If section is missing or does not correspond to any known section, a default section is used for focus, but all content remains visible. (2) leaderId: represents the currently selected leadership profile. The leadership directory and leadership detail components read leaderId from the URL after getAboutPageContent finishes loading. They use this value to locate the matching entry in leadership_team using a deterministic mapping (such as an index or derived identifier based on properties provided in leadership_team). If leaderId matches a leadership entry, that entry is set as selected and its detailed information is shown in the detail component. If leaderId is absent or invalid, no specific leader is selected by default (or a default selection rule may be applied consistently). Changes in selection from user clicks update leaderId in the URL to keep state shareable. (3) Other parameters: any unspecified query parameters are ignored by this page's logic. The getAboutPageContent interface has no input parameters, so no query parameter affects what data is fetched; they only affect how already-fetched data is presented. Outgoing navigation to contact.html explicitly sets inquiry_type=general_inquiry in the URL so the contacted page can interpret user intent. All state is thus recoverable from a combination of getAboutPageContent results and the URL query parameters.

components.functionality: Calls getAboutPageContent when the About page initializes (and optionally on manual refresh) and provides the loaded data to child components via a shared state or context.
event_handlers: onInit: invoke getAboutPageContent and store the returned object in page-level state.
onRefreshContent: re-invoke getAboutPageContent, replace existing state with new data, and notify subscribed components.

components.functionality: Manages the in-page navigation between sections (overview, history, mission, values, stats, certifications, leadership) and synchronizes the active section with the section query parameter in the URL.
event_handlers: onInit: read URL.searchParams.section to set the initial active section key; if missing or invalid, fall back to a default section key.
onClickSectionLink: update URL.searchParams.section to the selected section key using history.pushState or a full page reload, then trigger scroll or focus to the corresponding section.
onURLChange: when the URL changes (e.g., via back/forward navigation), update the active section key and notify relevant content sections to adjust focus.

components.functionality: Displays the company overview text returned by getAboutPageContent and can be targeted by in-page navigation as the overview section.
event_handlers: onSectionActivated: when the active section key becomes the overview key, ensure this section is scrolled into view or focused.
onContentUpdate: when getAboutPageContent.company_overview changes (e.g., on refresh), re-render the overview content.

components.functionality: Displays the company history text returned by getAboutPageContent and acts as the target section for history-related navigation.
event_handlers: onSectionActivated: respond when the active section key corresponds to the history section by scrolling into view or focusing this section.
onContentUpdate: re-render the history content when getAboutPageContent.history changes.

components.functionality: Displays the company mission text returned by getAboutPageContent and acts as the target for mission navigation.
event_handlers: onSectionActivated: respond when the active section key corresponds to the mission section by scrolling into view or focusing this section.
onContentUpdate: re-render the mission content when getAboutPageContent.mission changes.

components.functionality: Renders the list of company values using the values array from getAboutPageContent, showing each value's title and description.
event_handlers: onSectionActivated: when the active section key corresponds to the values section, scroll or focus into view.
onContentUpdate: when the values array changes, re-render the list, iterating over each item’s title and description.

components.functionality: Displays a set of key statistics about the company using the stats array returned by getAboutPageContent, showing each label and value.
event_handlers: onSectionActivated: when the active section key corresponds to the stats section, ensure this summary is visible or focused.
onContentUpdate: when stats change, re-render the summary items using the label and value properties.

components.functionality: Displays the company’s certifications using the certifications array from getAboutPageContent, including name, issuing body, and description for each item.
event_handlers: onSectionActivated: when the active section key corresponds to the certifications section, scroll or focus this list into view.
onContentUpdate: when the certifications array changes, re-render the certifications list using each item’s fields.

components.functionality: Displays the leadership_team array as a selectable list; manages which leader is currently selected based on user clicks and the leaderId query parameter.
event_handlers: onInit: after getAboutPageContent.leadership_team is available, read URL.searchParams.leaderId and attempt to match it to one entry (e.g., by index or derived identifier). If a match is found, mark that leader as selected.
onSectionActivated: if the active section key corresponds to the leadership section, ensure the directory is visible or focused.

onClickLeaderItem: set the clicked entry as the selected leader in local state, update URL.searchParams.leaderId to a corresponding identifier, and ensure URL.searchParams.section indicates the leadership section.
onURLChange: if leaderId changes due to browser navigation, update the selected leader to match the new leaderId parameter.

components.functionality: Shows detailed information (name, title, bio, and photo_url) for the currently selected leadership profile, determined by the selection state managed by the leadership-directory component and synchronized with leaderId.
event_handlers: onSelectedLeaderChange: when the selection in the leadership-directory changes (or leaderId in the URL changes), re-resolve the selected leadership_team entry and update the detail view content.
onContentUpdate: if getAboutPageContent.leadership_team changes (e.g., on refresh), re-evaluate the current leaderId against the new data and update or clear the detail view accordingly.

components.functionality: Provides a single call-to-action that sends users from the About page to the contact page with an appropriate query parameter for a general inquiry.
event_handlers: onClickContactCTA: navigate to "contact.html?inquiry_type=general_inquiry" while preserving or discarding other query parameters as defined by navigation rules (this navigation sets inquiry_type to general_inquiry as specified in the page architecture).

layout.grid_system: Implicit 12‑column style grid with full-width header bands and a centered content column inside max-width constraints.
layout.layout_pattern: All About Us components sit inside the existing site chrome: the light grey utility bar, black header, and dark grey global navigation stay unchanged at the top 20–25% of the viewport. The About Us page content begins as a single, centered white band below the global navigation. Within this white area, components are stacked vertically in a linear, top‑to‑bottom flow, following a single-column layout aligned to a 12‑column grid. Most sections span roughly 8–10 of the 12 columns, centered, leaving generous left/right margins. The only deliberate break from pure single-column is in the leadership area where a two-column split (directory + detail) appears within the same centered container, still respecting the main grid.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: All About Us components sit inside the existing site chrome: the light grey utility bar, black header, and dark grey global navigation stay unchanged at the top 20–25% of the viewport. The About Us page content begins as a single, centered white band below the global navigation. Within this white area, components are stacked vertically in a linear, top‑to‑bottom flow, following a single-column layout aligned to a 12‑column grid. Most sections span roughly 8–10 of the 12 columns, centered, leaving generous left/right margins. The only deliberate break from pure single-column is in the leadership area where a two-column split (directory + detail) appears within the same centered container, still respecting the main grid.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The white content band occupies the remaining 75–80% of the viewport height below the header stack. Within this band, the top portion (roughly the first 20–25% of the content area) is dedicated to the About section navigation and the company overview (including any hero image). The middle 50–60% of the content area is used for company history, mission, values, stats, and certifications, stacked with clear vertical separation. The lower 20–30% is reserved for leadership details and the prominent contact CTA. Standard vertical spacing between major sections is 32–48px, while internal spacing between headings, paragraphs, and list items uses 8–24px increments. Content width is constrained to a maximum (e.g., ~960–1140px) and typically uses about 70–80% of the available page width on large screens, maintaining comfortable side margins.
layout_strategies.content_density: The content is moderately dense in informational sections but visually relieved by consistent white space. Text-heavy sections (overview, history, mission) use a readable line length (around 60–70 characters per line) with 24–32px of padding above and below each block to avoid crowding. Lists (values, certifications) and stats are slightly denser, with items grouped more tightly vertically but separated by clear 8–16px gaps and, where needed, faint dividers. The leadership area balances density: a compact, scrollable list of names on the left and a more spacious detail view on the right. The contact CTA at the bottom is visually open with generous padding to stand out as a clear action point.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: Within the About page, the eye follows a Z‑pattern similar to the mockup: starting at the top-left of the white content area with the About section navigation and page heading, scanning horizontally across the in-page navigation tabs, then dropping down into the company overview and large central visual. From there, the user scrolls down through clearly segmented sections in a linear path. In the leadership area, the eye first hits the left column directory list, then moves right to the leadership detail panel, moving back and forth as different leaders are selected. The contact CTA anchors the bottom of the page with strong red accents, giving a clear visual endpoint and path to the contact page.

layout.overall_description: Below the existing stacked header (light grey utility bar, black brand bar, and dark grey global navigation), the About Us page opens into a clean, white content band that fills the rest of the viewport. Centered within this band is a constrained content column aligned to a 12‑column grid, leaving wide left and right margins that echo the corporate, high-contrast look from the mockup. At the very top of this white area, a slim in-page About navigation strip spans most of the width, offering quick anchors to Overview, History, Mission, Values, Statistics, Certifications, and Leadership. Directly beneath, the company overview section introduces the firm, typically with a wide landscape image centered in the column and a block of left-aligned copy beneath it. As the user scrolls, sections follow one after another with consistent vertical spacing: history and mission in text blocks, a more structured values list, a horizontal summary of key statistics, and a list of certifications. Deeper in the page, the layout briefly shifts into a two-column structure for leadership: a compact list of names on the left and a detailed profile area on the right, both still within the centered white band. Finally, a strong, horizontally centered contact call-to-action closes the page, using red accents and generous padding to stand out against the otherwise white content area while still feeling integrated with the overall banded, corporate aesthetic.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The data loader sits logically at the top of the About page’s white content band, but it appears as a full-column overlay only while content is being fetched. When active, it covers the entire centered content column from just below the dark grey global navigation bar down to approximately the first full viewport height of the white area. The overlay uses a slightly translucent white or very light grey background so the underlying structure is hinted but visually muted. A simple, flat loading indicator (spinner or progress bar) is centered horizontally and placed slightly above the vertical midpoint (around 40% from the top of the visible white area), consistent with the compact, utilitarian feel of the header. Padding around the loader content follows the base spacing system (roughly 32px from the top and sides of the content column), keeping it aligned with the grid and margins of other sections.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Behaves as an absolute-positioned overlay constrained to the main content column; it is fixed relative to the scrolling content area while active, blocking interaction with underlying sections until loading completes.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The About section navigation is the first visible component in the white content area, positioned directly below the dark grey global navigation bar with a slim 16–24px gap to maintain separation between site-level and page-level controls. It sits as a horizontal strip centered within the content column, spanning roughly 80–90% of the column width (about 8–10 grid columns), with 12–16px of vertical padding inside. The background remains white or a very light grey to keep it subtle, while each nav item is rendered as a flat, uppercase text tab with 16–24px horizontal padding and 8–12px vertical padding. Tabs are arranged left-to-right along the top of the content column, starting from the left margin, with the active section highlighted through a red underline or bottom border spanning the width of the text. The overall strip height is modest (approximately 48–56px), matching the compact utility feel of the upper header bands and creating a clear horizontal line that guides the eye before the user scrolls into deeper content.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Configured as a sticky element within the white content area: as the user scrolls down, it pins to the top of the viewport just beneath the global site navigation, maintaining quick access to section anchors without leaving the grid or overlapping header bars.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The company overview section sits immediately below the About section navigation, separated by about 32px of vertical space. It occupies the main centered content column, spanning roughly 70–80% of the page width on large screens (about 8–10 of 12 grid columns) and aligned centrally with equal left and right margins. At the top of the section, a left-aligned section title (e.g., “Company Overview”) appears with 8–16px of space above and below it. Directly beneath the heading, a large landscape image or hero visual is centered horizontally, occupying roughly 60–70% of the content column width and maintaining a 16–24px margin from the side edges. This image takes up a noticeable vertical portion of the section, echoing the main news image from the mockup. Below the image, the overview text forms a single column of left-aligned paragraphs with a line length of about 60–70 characters, spaced with 16px line spacing and 16–24px between paragraphs. The bottom of the section leaves 32–40px of white space before the next section, maintaining the airy feel observed in the main content region of the mockup.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the scrollable flow; content reflows within the centered column. On narrower viewports, the image scales down to the full width of the content column with text flowing beneath.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The company history section is placed directly beneath the overview, with approximately 40–48px of vertical separation to clearly indicate a new segment. It stays within the same centered content column width (about 8–10 grid columns) and maintains left-aligned text to reinforce the formal, corporate tone. At the top, a bold section heading sits flush with the left edge of the content column. Below the heading, the history content is arranged as one or more paragraphs stacked vertically, possibly broken into sub-periods with subtle subheadings. Each paragraph block is separated by 16px of vertical space, and any dates or milestones are either bolded inline or grouped as a simple vertical list with 8–12px spacing between items. The overall section height is moderate, aiming for clarity without excessive vertical sprawl, and a consistent 32–40px gap at the bottom leads into the mission section.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, scrollable content that adheres to the same single-column layout; no special fixed or floating behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The mission section follows the history section with around 32–40px of vertical spacing. It continues within the same centered column width but is given slightly more visual emphasis through subtle styling. The top includes a left-aligned heading (e.g., “Our Mission”) followed by either a short lead-in line or the mission statement itself set in a slightly larger or bolder type. The primary mission text is constrained to a single column with a comfortable line length and may be visually separated from adjacent sections by a light grey background block or a thin red vertical accent line along the left edge of the content (inside the main column), extending the height of the mission text. Internal padding of 24–32px around this mission block ensures it feels like a focused, important statement while still fitting into the structured, rectangular shapes of the overall design. A 32–40px margin below the mission block separates it from the values list that follows.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static; remains within the linear scroll. If a background block is used, it stretches only across the content column, not full viewport width, preserving the centered band feeling.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The values list section appears below the mission with a vertical gap of about 32–40px. It begins with a left-aligned heading and a brief introductory line, then transitions into a structured list of core values. Within the main centered column (about 8–10 grid columns wide), values are laid out in either a two-column grid (on larger screens) or a single stacked column (on smaller screens). In the two-column layout, each value item occupies roughly 45–48% of the content width, with a 16–24px horizontal gutter between columns and 16–24px vertical spacing between rows. Each item is presented as a compact rectangular block or card: a value title at the top (possibly bold or slightly larger), an optional simple icon or bullet on the far left, and a short description underneath. Padding inside each item follows the 16px base, giving each value room without appearing overly spacious. The bottom of the values section again leaves about 32–40px before the stats summary.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static list that flows within the column; on narrow screens, columns collapse into a single vertical list, preserving consistent left alignment.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The stats summary sits beneath the values section with approximately 32px of separation. It is designed as a horizontally oriented band within the main content column, emphasizing key numbers. The section heading is left-aligned at the top, with a modest 8–12px gap before the stats themselves. Below the heading, three to four stat blocks are arranged side by side in a single row, each occupying roughly 20–25% of the content width with even spacing between them. Each stat block is a flat rectangular area with a large, bold number (potentially in red for emphasis) at the top and a concise label beneath, both center-aligned within the block. Vertical padding inside each block is about 16–24px, making the band feel tight and information-heavy but still readable. All stat blocks share a consistent height to create a strong horizontal line across the page, echoing the banded header structure above. A 32–40px gap below the row of stats separates this dense information band from the certifications list.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static horizontal layout inside the column; on narrower viewports, stat blocks stack into two columns or a single column while maintaining equal spacing.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The certifications list section follows the stats summary with a 32–40px vertical gap. It remains within the central column width and uses a straightforward, vertically stacked layout. At the top, a left-aligned heading introduces the section. Below, certifications are presented as a simple list: each item spans the full width of the content column and includes the certification name, issuing body, and optionally a small icon or logo aligned to the left. Items are separated by 12–16px of vertical space, and faint horizontal rules can be used between items to enhance the structured, slightly formal feel. If logos are present, they are kept small and aligned in a neat column to preserve the clean rectangular rhythm. The section maintains moderate vertical height and leaves approximately 40px of space at the bottom before transitioning into the leadership area.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static vertical list that scrolls with the page; all items remain within the main centered column without special positioning.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The leadership directory marks a subtle shift in layout within the same centered content band. Positioned beneath the certifications section with about 40px of vertical separation, it introduces a two-column structure. The entire leadership area still respects the main content width (around 8–10 grid columns), but within it, the left 30–35% of the width is dedicated to the directory list while the right 65–70% is reserved for the detail panel. The directory column sits flush with the left edge of the content column. It contains a vertical list of leader names, each row spanning the full width of this left column. Each entry is compact: a name in bold, a role subtitle below, and optional small thumbnail aligned to the far left. Rows are separated by 8–12px vertical spacing with 12–16px internal padding per row. The currently selected leader is indicated by a strong red accent (such as a left border bar or a bold text color) and possibly a light grey background. If the list is long, the directory area can scroll internally within its allocated height, which roughly aligns with the visible height of the detail panel to its right, maintaining a clean horizontal alignment across the section.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static in the page flow but internally scrollable if needed; it remains locked to the left side of the leadership section while the user scrolls the overall page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The leadership detail panel occupies the right portion of the leadership area, starting at the same vertical position as the directory and aligned with the top of its first row. It spans roughly 65–70% of the leadership section width and is fully contained within the main centered content column. The panel appears as a flat, white rectangle with a faint grey border or subtle shadow to create separation from the background and the directory list. Inside, the leader’s name is prominently displayed at the top left, with the title and department just below. The upper-left portion of the panel may contain a larger headshot aligned with the text, with the bio and additional details flowing in a single column to the right of or below the image, depending on available width. Generous internal padding (24–32px) ensures the content feels open, while 16–24px spacing is used between text blocks (bio, experience, certifications). The bottom of the panel aligns visually with the bottom of the directory column where possible, creating a solid horizontal baseline before the final contact CTA section.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the page; content inside scrolls with the page rather than being independently scrollable, ensuring the leadership detail feels integrated with the overall About page flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The contact CTA is the final component on the page, positioned directly beneath the leadership section with a more generous vertical separation of about 48px to mark it as the concluding action. It appears as a horizontally centered band within the white content area, spanning the full width of the main content column (approximately 8–10 grid columns) and visually distinguished with either a light grey background block or a subtle black accent line at its top edge. Inside this band, content is center-aligned: a short, bold headline (e.g., “Ready to Discuss Your Project?”) sits near the top, a brief supporting line sits 8–12px below, and a primary red button is placed directly beneath, all stacked vertically. Vertical padding of 24–32px above and below the text/button creates a strong, open visual anchor. The button itself uses the brand red with white text, rectangular with slightly softened corners, and 10–12px vertical / 24–32px horizontal padding, matching the button style implied by the header utilities. The CTA leaves a final 32–40px of white space before the end of the content band, providing a clean visual finish.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static at the bottom of the scrollable content; no sticky behavior, but visually acts as the terminal call-to-action for the page.
